source: Claims against Malayan Government. Requisitioning of ships__CO273:670:11
filename: Claims against Malayan Government. Requisitioning of ships__CO273:670:11.txt
summary:
- File concerns **claims against the Malayan/Straits Settlements Government (and related Hong Kong Government claims)** arising from **wartime requisitioning/chartering of ships and small craft**, including questions of **hire, compensation for loss, and unpaid freight** (mainly **1941–1944**, with heavy discussion in **1943**).  
- **Geography/timeframe:** Far East/South-East Asia—especially **Singapore and Malaya** (fall of Singapore cited as **15 Feb 1942**), plus **Hong Kong** (fall **25 Dec 1941**), with references to voyages involving **Bangkok, Colombo, India, Australia, Macao, Shanghai**.  
- Key actors: **Colonial Office (Eastern Dept.)**, **Ministry of War Transport (MWT)** (notably H.C. Boyle, W.H.B. Court, W.A. Ward), **Crown Agents**, **Treasury Solicitor/Legal Adviser**, **Trading with the Enemy Department**, and claimants including **John Swire & Sons / Tientsin Lighter Co.**, **Glen Line / Straits Steamship Co.**, **Norwegian Shipping & Trade Mission (Nortraship)**, **Mr. Mak Man Sang**, and **Lambert Brothers (Insurance) Ltd**.  
- Major legal/policy issue: impact of the **House of Lords “Waalhaven” case** (summarised as **Sovfracht v. Van Udens (1943) A.C. 203**) leading to caution about **paying claims where claimants/companies are resident or registered in enemy-occupied territory**; preference to **register claims** rather than pay until circumstances permit, unless claimant is **registered in the U.K.** (e.g., under **Defence (Companies) Regulations 1940**).  
- **Swire lighters (A2 & A4):** Colonial Office reverses earlier stance and decides to **pay compensation** for presumed loss of two lighters hired to Straits Settlements Government; settlement discussed around **£2,560 per lighter** (total **£5,120**), with valuation debate (pre-war value cited **£2,400**; Swire argued for higher figure).  
- **Norwegian Shipping & Trade Mission freight claims:**  
  - **s.s. “Hellas”**—freight claim in **Hong Kong dollars**; internal analysis lists freight charges totaling **HK$97,433.40** (with breakdown including **East Asiatic Co. $3,897** and rice-related amounts). Although an **ex gratia payment** was proposed/processed in mid-1943, later correspondence shows **dispute over whether teak cargo was requisitioned** and whether freight on teak is a Straits Settlements liability; ultimately the claim (at least in part) is treated as **for registration**, with registration explicitly stated as **not admitting liability**.  
  - **“Mabella”**—voyage freight **Straits $24,454.50** (sterling equivalent discussed around **£2,850–£2,902** depending on exchange); uncertainty whether payment was already credited locally via **Boustead & Co.** leads to decision leaning toward **registration for post-reoccupation settlement**, despite concern about possible international complications.  
- **Glen Line / Straits Steamship Co. requisition hire & war risk premiums:** discussion of claims for **15 vessels** believed requisitioned by Straits Settlements Government around **early Jan 1942**; only **7** directly evidenced but CO prepared to assume all were requisitioned. Straits Steamship Co. later **registered in the U.K. (19 Aug 1943)** as **Singapore Straits Steamship Company Ltd**, removing a key barrier to payment. War risk insurance premiums cited as **£5,465 8s. 1d.** (said to cover **91 days**), with Crown Agents advising Straits Settlements Government liable only for the **proportion** covering its charter period; CO drafts propose limiting Straits liability to **15 Feb 1942** and seeking MWT to assume liability thereafter for certain ships (e.g., **Angby, Klang, Perak**).  
- Additional/related matters: **Mr. Mak Man Sang** (ships **Hareldawins** and **Marion**)—ownership/title uncertain; earlier **HK$3,000 per month for three months** authorised as maintenance/advance; claim viewed as likely barred by the House of Lords ruling and therefore to be **registered**, with **Lambert Bros.** asked to submit details. **s.s. “Taiposhan”**—MWT indicates requisition by **Hong Kong Government** in early 1941; possible claim for hire up to **25 Dec 1941** (fall of Hong Kong) to be **registered unless satisfactory proof** provided.
